en_title: Like The Heart Of A Newly Embroidered Kerchief
en_body:
.......................................

a square cloth
threads curling in embroidered colors
your name, the letters
embrace the waist of mine
clouds veil the bright sky
square kerchief,
what kerchief slips into a pocket
waiting to wipe rose-colored tears
a reach of river
curving
its embracing circle
the bridge leans a shoulder
calling remembrance-longing
 misted dew
why hurry with such swift steps
 toward shore
linger here a little
so love
remembers deep

kerchief embroidered complete
softly
passed into your hand
mid-bridge of remembrance
 hold the kerchief
do not let it fall

 kerchief like the heart
 choosing every color of thread
 yet love returns to ache
 like the needle embroidering love:

...whom does the kerchief long for
 the kerchief falls upon the earth
 whom does the kerchief long for
 the kerchief drapes across the shoulder...**

.....................................

**Vietnamese ca-dao
* inspired by the title of a
 love-song by
 TrinhCongSon

like the heart of a newly embroidered kerchief*

.......................................

a square cloth
threads curling in embroidered colors
your name, the letters
embrace the waist of mine
clouds veil the bright sky
square kerchief,
what kerchief slips into a pocket
waiting to wipe rose-colored tears
a reach of river
curving
its embracing circle
the bridge leans a shoulder
calling remembrance-longing
 misted dew
why hurry with such swift steps
 toward shore
linger here a little
so love
remembers deep

kerchief embroidered complete
softly
passed into your hand
mid-bridge of remembrance
 hold the kerchief
do not let it fall

 kerchief like the heart
 choosing every color of thread
 yet love returns to ache
 like the needle embroidering love:

...whom does the kerchief long for
 the kerchief falls upon the earth
 whom does the kerchief long for
 the kerchief drapes across the shoulder...**

.....................................

**Vietnamese ca-dao
* inspired by the title of a
 love-song by
 TrinhCongSon

1
note: The refrain comes from a Vietnamese ca-dao, and the title alludes to a love-song by Trịnh Công Sơn.
